**_POLITICO: Trump 'all in' on Senate Obamacare repeal_**
We need your attention, team -- because this is too important.

While all eyes are on Washington because of James Comey's testimony, Trump is working behind the scenes to pass Obamacare repeal through the Senate. **_POLITICO_ just reported:**

> _" With the party the closest it's been in seven years to repeal President Barack Obama's signature law, Donald Trump is now pushing for action -- and a win that could finally put the GOP's agenda back on track."_

**It 's true: In a meeting with Senate Republicans, Trump demanded an "an aggressive timetable" from Mitch McConnell.** Then, he dined with more GOP Senators that evening to pressure them to follow suit.

The last time Trump got involved and strong armed House Republicans to get on board for Obamacare repeal -- it passed in 36 hours.

name: Jason Yates, My Faith Votes
subject: Kirk Cameron, Dr. Ben Carson & K-LOVE
body:
Hang out w/ Kirk Cameron, & we'll pay for everything!

View this email in your browser

Dear Peter,

Did you attend Revive US last year, our
one-of-a-kind, LIVE event with Kirk Cameron? Hundreds of
thousands of you attended in theaters across the nation for this
interactive faith experience.

And guess what… we are doing it again!

On October 17th you can be a part of this
all-new, one-night-only, LIVE event tackling the tough issues we
face on a daily basis, and we are simply calling it Revive US 2.

But what's better than watching it in the
theater? Being on set with Kirk Cameron and the other amazing
artists and guests!

With My Faith Votes' exclusive VIP pass,
you'll be hanging with Kirk!

But we will make it even BETTER…My
Faith Votes will cover all your expenses. That's
right…airfare, hotel, food, VIP access...we've got it all
covered for you.*

So don't wait another second! Enter before
July 31 to win this amazing, once-in-a-lifetime experience, or
you can also text the word REVIVE to 67076 to enter to win.

We were thrilled to partner with K-LOVE Fan
Awards to announce the Revive US 2 giveaway. The K-LOVE Fan
Awards Show is far better than any secular awards show, and you
have a chance to watch it.

For one night only, June 13th, you can go to
select theaters to see what we saw live. Many of the theaters are
selling out, so go here to reserve your tickets. SECRET TIP: Dr.
Carson was a part of the show…you won't want to miss it!

We want to thank K-LOVE for the positive and
uplifting message of faith and the impact they have had on our
nation.

Faith makes America stronger,

Jason Yates
CEO
My Faith Votes

My Faith Votes is a non-partisan movement
focused on motivating Christians in America to participate in
local and national elections and be active members of society. By
partnering with local churches, pastors and national faith
leaders, My Faith Votes mobilizes and equips Christians to lead
the conversation on the place of faith in culture and politics.

*See official rules of the Revive US 2
Giveaway for details.

name: Melania Trump
subject: The President’s Birthday Card
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trump Make America Great Again Committee, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Donald J
body:
A very special birthday is approaching... |
[](http://click1.action.gop.com/umnzpbqjfdysnbnbsgkgqsvqmvsjmmgkzwngwhvhwggqfq_kdhlddfsstv_ozmmrrwt.html?a=1260&b=87751&c=17786605)

Peter,

I wanted to save reaching out to you for a very special event.

On June 14, 2017, President Trump will turn 71, and I need your help to make it a birthday my husband will never forget.

Our celebrations always include family and close friends.

You have certainly proven yourself to be a loyal friend of my husband through your support of his positive vision to Make America Great Again and your unwavering commitment to the Republican Party.

**[Please, join me in celebrating Donald's 71st birthday by signing the card before the deadline, 9:00 PM on June 14, 2017.](http://click1.action.gop.com/vmgqjbvcgtrnhbhbnkzkvndvmdncmmkzqphkpfdfpkkvgp_kdhlddfsstv_ihzznnmy.html?a=1260&b=87751&c=17786605)

And to make it even more special, be sure to include your personal message.**

Peter, Donald will be glad to hear from friends like you who have meant so much to his and our Party's success.

[**So please, take a moment to sign the President 's birthday card. Your support and kind wishes mean so much to our family.**](http://click1.action.gop.com/qhpjpqwcfbntgqgqtvrvwtywhytchhvrjsgvsmymsvvwfv_kdhlddfsstv_khffddvl.html?a=1260&b=87751&c=17786605)

Warmly,

[](http://click1.action.gop.com/wzztnqlhsyfbgqgqbwmwlbklzkbhzzwmtvgwvdkdvwwlsg_kdhlddfsstv_blbbccgp.html?a=1260&b=87751&c=17786605)
Melania Trump

[SIGN THE CARD](http://click1.action.gop.com/zdfgwjmcltzksjsjkbqbmkvmdvkcddbqgpsbprvrpbbmll_kdhlddfsstv_qwhhppgm.html?a=1260&b=87751&c=17786605)

Paid for by Trump Make America Great Again Committee, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Donald J. Trump for President, Inc. and the Republican National Committee.
You are receiving this email at dpwillis67@gmail.com
Trump Make America Great Again Committee, 138 Conant Street, 2nd Floor, Beverly, MA 01915

We believe this is an important way to reach our grassroots supporters with the most up-to-date information regarding the efforts of the Trump Make America Great Again Committee and President Trump, and we're glad you're on our team. It's because of grassroots supporters like you that we will Make America Great Again, and we appreciate your support. Thank you for all that you do!

[Privacy Policy](http://click1.action.gop.com/qdnjpqwcfbntgqgqtvrvwtywhytchhvrjsgvsmymsvvwfp_kdhlddfsstv_xvssjjrd.html?a=17786605) | [Unsubscribe](http://click1.action.gop.com/hfvlhrqdwgnzcrcrzspsqzjqmjzdmmsplbcsbvjvbssqwm_kdhlddfsstv_rrwwttgc.html?a=17786605)

**Trump Picks King Of Voter Suppression To Lead 'Election Integrity' Commission**
\- _Huffington Post_ , 5/15 Friends,

President Donald Trump created a taxpayer-funded commission to investigate his absurd claims of voter fraud.

This comes as Republicans are working feverishly to impose stricter voter identification laws all across the country. **These laws have an outsized impact on the ability of people of color as well as poorer individuals to access the ballot box.**

Leading Trump's commission is a man the ACLU has dubbed the '**King of Voter Suppression**.' Kris Kobach made headlines as Kansas' Secretary of State for trying to impose some of the strictest voting restrictions of our time. His efforts and policies have been repeatedly struck down by state and federal courts for being brazen attempts at disenfranchising eligible voters.

[_**We 're organizing a joint action calling on Congress to reject Trump's voter suppression efforts. Add your name and reject Trump's reckless attack on voting rights.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113ef7/622b7b5b/3692dc1/307bc836/1459040327/VEsE/ "https://www.signherenow.org/petition/voter-fraud/dws/")

With Kobach at the head of the commission, it is clear that Trump is just trying to prop up his false claims to revitalize continued voter suppression efforts nationwide.

In this day and age, under this administration, voting rights are under attack. It is crucial that we defend voting rights and access to the ballot box.

[_**Call on Congress to reject Trump 's voter suppression efforts.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113ef7/622b7b5b/3692dc1/307bc836/1459040327/VEsF/ "https://www.signherenow.org/petition/voter-fraud/dws/")

Thank you,

Debbie |

In Case You Missed It:

"Gov vetoes budget, will Dems let union control them?"

By Emerson Lynn
St. Albans Messenger
June 7, 2017

As expected, Gov. Phil Scott vetoed the budget Tuesday, saying the spending plan is too rich for Vermonters’ pocketbooks.

At the same time, the administration is sending a letter to all its state agencies and departments to put together budgets that reflect cuts of two, three and four percent.

This follows an unanticipated 10 percent drop in the state’s revenue numbers for April, something that may prompt a revenue downgrade in July, when the new fiscal year begins.

In other words, the state’s economic doldrums continue, and, as the governor said in his veto message, “Vermont faces an immediate and growing crisis of affordability....”

The focus is on the $26 million the state could save if, as the governor proposes, the state would negotiate the teachers’ health care plan. But it’s more than that. It’s about state government’s ability to adapt to quickly changing demographics, and the disruptiveness that surrounds us. It’s about the state’s ability to resist the inertia that keeps us in the same ruts.

The teachers’ union has made a huge mistake in placing all its political capital behind a cause that not only is self-centered, but one that doesn’t recognize the need to adapt, to be part of the essential conversation as to how the state’s educational picture needs to change.

Its political backers are making the same mistake.

The governor’s veto is expected to be sustained. The Republicans [and some blue dog Democrats] have the votes. Beyond that it’s unclear how things will evolve. The governor has said he would not shut down state government if the Democrats hold firm and refuse to negotiate. But the Democrats need to be leery of being seen as lap dogs for the teachers’ union – that’s a tiny base from which to campaign going forward, and not a popular one.

The time between the Legislature’s adjournment and the governor’s veto message was used by the union and its adherents to paint the governor as being a clone of other anti-union forces, and someone who differs little from the Republicans in Congress and the president.

The strategy was to subtract from the governor’s strength, and his popularity.

That’s gone nowhere.

The governor holds the upper hand. Still. The public understands teachers are essentially being held harmless, and the fact that they would be negotiating with the state rather than local school boards for their health care isn’t seen as a negative to most Vermonters. What they have is a value most Vermonters don’t have.

The $26 million is also being played down as not a lot of money, something that would hardly register on the property tax bill of the average Vermonter.

But that’s $26 million each year the cut would go into full effect. Have we gotten to the point where $26 million each year is not worth consideration? $130 million over five years is nothing? Little wonder the average Vermonter is feeling pressed, anddisgusted.

So, yes, the governor has put himself in the position of having to accede to the union’s wish, and to the demand of the Democrats behind the union, just to keep state government functioning. But he will also be able to point to them as being the sole reason those savings are not being realized and why Vermont continues to become less affordable with each passing year.

He has done what he said he would do, which is to refuse to support a budget that would raise the property tax. There can’t be a Vermonter – of either party – who doesn’t know that. The Democrats are allowing the union to color everything they’ve done. They will be the ones remembered for denying Vermonters the $26 million in savings. They will be the ones remembered for pushing the state’s costs up, making us a more costly place to live.

As the state moves forward to deal with declining revenues, and as it adjusts the budget needs of each state agency and department, the $26 million sits in the backs of the voters’ minds. It’s more attitude than anything else, and something they won’t forget.

That’s unfortunate for all involved. Including the teachers.

It’s a hope against hope that the Democratic leadership will summon the moxie to tell the union to shift its focus to more important challenges, but we’ll hold that hope nonetheless.

name: Shannon Jackson, Our Revolution
subject: Join us at the Equality March
body:
members of the LGBTQ+ communities, their loved ones, and allies will gather in Washington, D.C.
#
Hi there,
The Equality March is happening this Sunday in Washington. LGBTQ+ communities, their loved ones, and allies are marching so that we can truly realize the promise of equality for all. For change to occur, we must join together and work as one. [**We need you at the Equality March this Sunday, June 11. RSVP to join us today.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0dab/43f17890/d00a025e/62037c28/2552126440/VEsH/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/event/detail/volunteeractivityormeeting/gpg57s?source=em170609-dc")
We look forward to having you with us.
--Shannon
* * *
Friends,
LGBTQ+ people have seen historic progress in the last decade. It's easy to assume we've overcome our biggest obstacles, or that the increased access to economic and civil rights for some of us is enough to satisfy our cry for justice for all. But there's still much more we must do. Every day our community faces hostile rhetoric, discriminatory policies, and violence.
This Sunday, June 11, members of the LGBTQ+ communities, their loved ones, and allies will gather in Washington, D.C., and cities across the country, to give voice to these concerns, and to support, uplift, and bring attention to those who have been actively silenced and neglected. The Equality March for Unity & Pride is a grassroots movement that will celebrate, inspire, and mobilize the diverse LGBTQ+ communities to clearly and peacefully oppose efforts to persecute and discriminate against LGBTQ+ individuals.
[**Will you stand up for LGBTQ+ equality by marching with Our Revolution at Sunday's Equality March for Unity & Pride?**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0dab/43f17890/d00a025e/62037c28/2552126440/VEsE/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/event/detail/volunteeractivityormeeting/gpg57s?source=em170609-dc")
[**Equality March for Unity & Pride**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0dab/43f17890/d00a025e/62037c28/2552126440/VEsF/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/event/detail/volunteeractivityormeeting/gpg57s?source=em170609-dc")
Farragut Square
17th St NW & I St NW, Washington, DC 20006
Sunday, June 11, 10:00 AM - 2:00 PM EDT
We're asking _you_ to join us, because it's our time. Our time to lift up the voices of marginalized people in our communities. Our time to stand up and be counted in this moment of political reckoning in the public consciousness. Our time to assert who we are and our values. name: Senator Maggie Hassan
subject: Comey's testimony
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Hello,
Yesterday, former FBI Director James Comey testified before the Senate Intelligence Committee about the Trump campaign's potential ties to Russia.
He underscored how significant Russian interference was in the 2016 election, and he highlighted the urgent need to uncover the truth.
Perhaps most concerning was his testimony that President Trump demanded the FBI Director's loyalty and attempted to influence an ongoing probe. **In fact, Comey went as far as to say that he believed he "was fired because of the Russia investigation."**
Comey's testimony is an important step, but the fact of the matter is that there are still more questions than answers around Trump's potential ties to Russia.
The American people deserve the truth, everyone. [**Add your name to commit to uncovering any connections Trump and his associates have to Russia and prevent any future attacks against our democracy.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fde8e/2a380d31/1b9b9659/fe9ddc/3774523639/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/s/20170609_ComeyTestimony?source=e20170609_comey1_act&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264")
Thank you.
With every good wish,
Senator Maggie Hassan

name: Democrats.org
subject: Don't let Paul Ryan get away with it (add your name)
body:
The GOP is trying to undo Wall Street reform
---
**House Republicans just snuck through a bill that would undo Wall Street reform and cripple the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau. Add your name right now to fight back:

** **[I'M IN](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Protect-Wall-Street-Reform1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+IM+IN&utm_campaign=em_20170609_dnc2_nd&source=em_20170609_dnc2_nd")**
Derek --

Ten years ago, an unregulated financial system preceded the worst recession since the Great Depression.

So President Obama and Democrats took action to reform Wall Street and create the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau to protect the American people and strengthen our economy.

**[But yesterday, House Republicans voted to undo Wall Street reform and cripple the CFPB. Add your name to stand with Democrats and fight back.](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Protect-Wall-Street-Reform1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+But+yesterday+House+Republicans+voted+to&utm_campaign=em_20170609_dnc2_nd&source=em_20170609_dnc2_nd")**

If Paul Ryan gets his way, he'd effectively turn Wall Street back into the Wild West. Big banks would be able to skirt the rules, and hardworking Americans would pay the price.

The House GOP snuck this vote through yesterday during James Comey's testimony in the Senate, probably thinking no one would notice. But we're not going to let Republicans get away with it.

**Make your voice heard -- tell Republicans to look out for hardworking families and keep Wall Street reform on the books. We've come too far to go back now:**

**[I'M IN](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Protect-Wall-Street-Reform1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+IM+IN&utm_campaign=em_20170609_dnc2_nd&source=em_20170609_dnc2_nd")**
Thanks,

The Democrats

**_Not Your Typical Party_**
We're going to be rolling out the red carpet - literally - on Tuesday, June 27, when we officially launch Team Holcomb. What's Team Holcomb? Well, you'll have to come and find out! But here's what we can promise: it's a game changing announcement that will impact the way we prepare for the upcoming election cycles. It's never been done before and other states who have caught wind of our plans are already trying to replicate. Yet again, Indiana is the blueprint!

We'll be joined by Governor **Eric Holcomb** , Lucas Oil Founder **Forrest Lucas** and tons of elected officials and candidates for the big announcement, not to mention the hit cover band, Dave and Rae.

Trust me, this won't be your typical fundraiser. No h'orderves, no boring speeches - just a lot of fun. Tickets start out at $50 for the general reception (a/k/a party) and go up to include a small round table gathering.

Governor Holcomb is fond of saying that you're not doing it right if you're not having fun. So we're going to do this right and have a lot of fun. Contact **Zach Willis** at zachwillis@holcombforindiana.com to reserve your spot or [click here for more information](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=e30217b829&e=1acd9bb3d6).

\- Kyle

(Photo: Chairman Hupfer chats with Governor Holcomb, Illinois State Senator **Bill Brady **and Illinois Republican Party State Chairman **Tim Schneider** during the McLean County Lincoln/Reagan Day Dinner on Wednesday.)
|
---
| | _**Governor Holcomb's Week That Was**_
---
|
---
| Only days before he leaves the borders of the United States for his first overseas trade mission as our state's chief executive, Governor Holcomb ventured outside Indiana's borders for a few hours to [speak at the McLean County Lincoln/Reagan Day Dinner in Bloomington, Illinois](http://indgop.us11.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=b8f172eecb&e=1acd9bb3d6), Wednesday night. The governor spoke about everyone's unique call to serve and how we need to expect the unexpected in both public service and our private lives - a lesson he first learned in the U.S. Navy. He also emphasized the importance of focusing on people in everything we do in governing and in politics. And, just like the company headquartered in Bloomington, State Farm, Governor Holcomb promised to be a "good neighbor" and to do our part to help Illinois get back on the right track. (But we still reserve the right to recruit more Illinois-based businesses to take root in Indiana!)

Governor Holcomb also traveled to Washington, D.C., for a [discussion on infrastructure at The White House](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=630f7a41e4&e=1acd9bb3d6) with President **Donald Trump** and Vice President **Mike Pence**. And there was a big announcement this week about the official expansion of Indiana's On My Way Pre-K program into 15 new counties. You can read all about that [here](http://indgop.us11.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=f2b81cf938&e=1acd9bb3d6).

Indiana's Auditor of State, Tera Klutz, who was appointed to the position by Governor Eric Holcomb in January, launched her official Facebook page this week. The governor spoke about everyone's unique call to serve and how we need to expect the unexpected in both public service and our private lives - a lesson he first learned in the U.S. Navy. He also emphasized the importance of focusing on people in everything we do in governing and in politics. And, just like the company headquartered in Bloomington, State Farm, Governor Holcomb promised to be a "good neighbor" and to do our part to help Illinois get back on the right track. (But we still reserve the right to recruit more Illinois-based businesses to take root in Indiana!) Governor Holcomb also traveled to Washington, D.C., for a with President Donald Trump and Vice President Mike Pence. And there was a big announcement this week about the official expansion of Indiana's On My Way Pre-K program into 15 new counties. You can read all about that . | | | Auditor Tera Klutz Launches Facebook Page - 'Like' Her Page Indiana's Auditor of State, Tera Klutz, who was appointed to the position by Governor Eric Holcomb in January, launched her official Facebook page this week. You can follow all the happenings in the Auditor's Office by clicking to 'Like' her page. She's doing a great job continuing and accelerating the legacy of transparency in the Auditor's Office and we know more great things are to come - so follow along! | | News You Can Use Senator Todd Young OpEd: Congresswoman Jackie Walorski WISH-TV : Events Calendar \- Ripley County Lincoln Day \- Crawford County Reagan Day Dinner Cruise with Congressman Trey Hollingsworth \- Southern Indiana Republican Golf Scramble \- Newton County Republican Golf Outing \- Hendricks County GOP Chairman's Breakfast with Secretary of State Connie Lawson \- SMCC Elephant Open Golf Outing \- Newton County Lincoln Day with Secretary of State Connie Lawson \- Hendricks County GOP Golf Outing \- Harrison County GOP Golf Scramble \- Fayette County GOP Hog Roast/BBQ \- Annual Buck Garden Party \- Perry-Spencer Republican Picnic | | | | "Everybody dreams of living in Hawaii, but have you considered the exotic splendor of Indiana?" We don't want to give away the rest - but it's GREAT news. | "Read More" | | Remember the "Illinoyed" campaign to lure folks from the Land of Lincoln to the great Hoosier State? Well, it's keeps working as more people cross the border. | "Read More" | | Congressman Larry Bucshon, for years a leader in the fight against opioid addiction and abuse, wrote an op-ed about his most recent work on the topic. | "Read More" | | | | | | | | | ---|--- | | | ---|--- | | | ---|--- | | | | | | | | | | | | | | mailto:info@indiana.gop | This email was sent to mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com Indiana Republican State Committee, Inc. 101 W. Ohio St., Suite 2200 Indianapolis, IN 46204 * USA

**NEW HAMPSHIRE REPUBLICAN PARTY**
**WEEKLY NEWSLETTER** FRIDAY, June 9th |
---|---|---
---
---
---
| | _**A Note From Jeanie**_ | |
---
| Dear Derek,
Last night I met with a group of Republican activists in Hopkinton who are ready to start their own town committee. I was excited about their commitment and enthusiasm to put a stake in the ground and grow Republican activism.
Their effort certainly fits within our [_**RED Plan**_](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=5MdO6cLza2MazVJVOHyurn2VWnLIAVAgPy-2BBq6oB94O-2BSXMx4CommrXGNnUUHGaR_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30M-2BjBdI1mX7fyfkq-2BTUH9i6Jks1bvpI2VrucNuwDvp9Kb-2B3G2atli096ghR5i7St8x-2B3WeMkG2uzRHQeh790PxLLi92zwiV9edHcsCwvpXeqd77L7-2F1C-2BwqBoW7Oq-2BbZPlrjmlPFmuAWuJYzl5-2FXZ-2FKBWpjMiCjv1hFCvAH7rDX17gtlF-2F6GPdLlX6xjLrFfliB92FEioKCe-2BDcN-2BGWSdq-2B899VAJub-2FDTY4Uc6uLix454KEJzydeS3WRqrjgjDEGj9W-2FkfUDmXwIwjz-2Bs6e2jWg-3D-3D)—a key component being to strengthen current Republican committees and grow new committees.
These folks understand the importance of building the grassroots now in preparation for 2018. They understand the Democrats are mad at their historic losses and will do everything they can to take back the corner office, and gain seats in the House and Senate.
If you’re reading this, and you’ve thought about wanting to get more involved or possibly starting your own town committee, [_**give us a call here at HQ**_](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=5MdO6cLza2MazVJVOHyurh6vpA2lNTXZ6J99c-2BOQVx2c9D8tPdvq-2BbH7RFzVnTE1_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30M-2BjBdI1mX7fyfkq-2BTUH9i6Jks1bvpI2VrucNuwDvp9Kb-2B3G2atli096ghR5i7St8x-2B3WeMkG2uzRHQeh790PxLATtJD1-2BXiOFN22gwaIED1JZ9U8X4PH-2FzkDYd3Lilzx6fGiArFTWXXpSUMdrXSlgyey1tNR-2FtCZ7kZWqk0EtvjAlZZYG9dq-2BO6xrXWjpASewwJ6GTufucfOCnFl1mmhSBv7CzF8oDj7Rk081dh5wm01xURHxXgHCfn5pnC1d5aSoH6RI1qb6zhe4d3Rfiw-2Bphg-3D-3D) and let us know how we can help make that happen.
Maybe you’ve thought about running for office? [_**Let us know—we can help!**_](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=5MdO6cLza2MazVJVOHyurm6bawHH7v9myhPM-2FXCp639DUv91eBwTJ80wd3Z5G9uB_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30M-2BjBdI1mX7fyfkq-2BTUH9i6Jks1bvpI2VrucNuwDvp9Kb-2B3G2atli096ghR5i7St8x-2B3WeMkG2uzRHQeh790PxLBjShawlJcD8K88cAsRUgFDKMo7Gtf8-2Fqj3HQ5Ogx0v1LwrJSH30FZJAYXgBJEwTiHuCh6Uh4nsd6JMEP3wjoSdLKkRIh1xsoc28l9OEKf9NmhzIuyBpytF5njKsDHd6pW5P604XijocXEh72Ryt341kk96-2BiHRKnwtoz8iaMPuS2iLpAmR2CzYyPllsdu6qKg-3D-3D)
_**[Check out our RED Plan](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=5MdO6cLza2MazVJVOHyurn2VWnLIAVAgPy-2BBq6oB94O-2BSXMx4CommrXGNnUUHGaR_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30M-2BjBdI1mX7fyfkq-2BTUH9i6Jks1bvpI2VrucNuwDvp9Kb-2B3G2atli096ghR5i7St8x-2B3WeMkG2uzRHQeh790PxLMP2KEWBrdYX42vLlYDnbadPxZdVKWCe-2FVDB4JTb20tkogL6Z0vA-2B9-2FcPJUCJucHNOrR78JjNs93JY86IZZXLpppL-2F-2F3pCqqKQEOMosfzRX-2BDnZ1M7GCvwCYjX6BbP-2B9tFoNJSp7igooaqA3Psvn1glBwbR2ric4BB1oBfyzf-2Fh3QmC2aoDjyX3yIxCNXonG5g-3D-3D)**_ and join us as we prepare to gain even more momentum going into 2018, growing our majorities in the House and Senate, and taking back the two Congressional seats.
Sincerely, _Jeanie_

**Rockingham 4 & Belknap 9 House Special Elections Called**
The Governor and Executive Council at their meeting on Wednesday, June 7th, called for a Special Election for State Representative for the Belknap 9 (Belmont & Laconia Wards 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 & 6) and Rockingham 4 (Auburn, Chester, & Sandown) House Districts. The Primary Election for the Belknap 9 District has been scheduled for Tuesday, September 12th, and the Special General Election has been scheduled for Tuesday, November 7th. If there is no primary for the seat then the Special General Election will be held on Tuesday, September 12th. The Primary Election for the Rockingham 4 District has been scheduled for Tuesday, August 8th and the Special General Election has been called for Tuesday, September 26th. If there is no primary for the seat then the Special General Election will be held on Tuesday, August 8th.
**Filing Period:** June 12 – 16, 2017. Candidates for both districts may file with their town clerks on Monday or Tuesday (12th or 13th) during normal business hours, OR candidates may file with the Secretary of State Monday-Friday. Any filings made on Friday, June 16th, must be made in person by the candidate at the Secretary of State’s Office. |

| _**Senate Republicans**_
**Bradley praises passage of ramped up support of drug interdiction efforts**
Concord, NH – Today, the New Hampshire Senate voted to concur with the House on SB 131, a bill establishing a cross border drug interdiction program using Fiscal Year 2017 funds.
Senate Majority Leader Jeb Bradley (R-Wolfeboro) issued the following statement: “This important legislation will help to cut off the flow of heroin, fentanyl and other deadly drugs that are poisoning Granite Staters every day,” said Senate Majority Leader Jeb Bradley (R-Wolfeboro), prime sponsor of the bill. “We continue to hear stories of arrests made at the Massachusetts border for selling fentanyl to dealers in New Hampshire. A cross border drug interdiction program will add the funds necessary to continue fighting this deadly epidemic.”
“The opioid crisis must be fought with an above all approach including prevention, treatment for addiction and working with law enforcement to get drug dealers off our streets. I am gratified by the good work of the House and look forward to this critical legislation proceeding to the Governor,” added Bradley.

* * *

**Avard praises signing of stronger rape shield protections for NH victims**
Concord, NH - Today, Governor Chris Sununu signed SB 9, a bill relative to the admissibility of prior consensual sexual activity in sexual assault cases.
Senator Kevin Avard (R-Nashua), the bill’s prime sponsor, issued the following statement after the bill was signed.
“Today’s signing of SB 9 into law serves to enhance protections for victims of sexual assault from being required to divulge additional unrelated sexual history in a trial or appeal.”
“The threat of being asked to reveal additional personal and unrelated information is a significant barrier for many victims of sexual assault that prevents them from coming forward. By enhancing protections that does now allow the admission of prior sexual history in a trial we are allowing victims the assurance that their private life can remain private.”
“I would like to thank the advocates who worked tirelessly on this legislation, for my colleagues support throughout the process and for Governor Sununu’s willingness to sign this measure into law today. We hope that removing this barrier will help victims seek the justice they deserve.”
PHOTO: Governor Chris Sununu signs SB 9 alongside Bob and Melissa Marriott, the mother and father Lizzi Marriott, and the bill’s prime sponsor, Senator Kevin Avard, advocates and supporters. |

Senate Majority Leader Jeb Bradley (R-Wolfeboro) issued the following statement: “This important legislation will help to cut off the flow of heroin, fentanyl and other deadly drugs that are poisoning Granite Staters every day,” said Senate Majority Leader Jeb Bradley (R-Wolfeboro), prime sponsor of the bill. “We continue to hear stories of arrests made at the Massachusetts border for selling fentanyl to dealers in New Hampshire. A cross border drug interdiction program will add the funds necessary to continue fighting this deadly epidemic.” “The opioid crisis must be fought with an above all approach including prevention, treatment for addiction and working with law enforcement to get drug dealers off our streets. I am gratified by the good work of the House and look forward to this critical legislation proceeding to the Governor,” added Bradley. Avard praises signing of stronger rape shield protections for NH victims Concord, NH - Today, Governor Chris Sununu signed SB 9, a bill relative to the admissibility of prior consensual sexual activity in sexual assault cases. Senator Kevin Avard (R-Nashua), the bill’s prime sponsor, issued the following statement after the bill was signed. “Today’s signing of SB 9 into law serves to enhance protections for victims of sexual assault from being required to divulge additional unrelated sexual history in a trial or appeal.” “The threat of being asked to reveal additional personal and unrelated information is a significant barrier for many victims of sexual assault that prevents them from coming forward. By enhancing protections that does now allow the admission of prior sexual history in a trial we are allowing victims the assurance that their private life can remain private.” “I would like to thank the advocates who worked tirelessly on this legislation, for my colleagues support throughout the process and for Governor Sununu’s willingness to sign this measure into law today. name: Shannon Jackson, Our Revolution
subject: House rolls back Dodd-Frank
body:
Senate Republicans are now poised to roll back Dodd-Frank and the Affordable Care Act in the same month
#
Friends,
While most of the country wasn't looking, House Republicans voted to claw back virtually every banking regulation implemented after Wall Street crashed and the Great Recession began. With so much scrutiny on the White House, Senate Republicans are now poised to roll back Dodd-Frank and the Affordable Care Act in the same month. Mitch McConnell, Paul Ryan, and every other politician bought by Wall Street and corporate donors thinks that they can get away with this while we're distracted by the daily circus in the West Wing.
We haven't forgotten what Wall Street deregulation got us last time. A reeling housing crisis, millions of people's life savings wiped out, and 800,000 jobs lost every month by the time George W. Bush left office. We remember Occupy, and the reprieve that the most egregious offenders got from going to prison. Executives at the largest financial firms who gambled people's hard-earned life savings away were treated less harshly than the people actually paying their mortgage.
We need to break the big banks up and implement a modern day version of Glass-Steagall, but we have to stop Republicans from rolling back even more Wall Street regulations first. [**Will you join our fight to protect Main Street from Wall Street greed? Sign our petition to tell Congress: Stop the Wall Street assault on working families.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0daf/43f17890/d08b5ea1/62037aa6/2389331383/VEsH/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/glass-steagall?source=em170609-full")
The Financial CHOICE Act wipes out the Dodd-Frank law signed in 2010, putting the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau in Donald Trump's control. That's right, the department charged with protecting consumers will be under the control of a person who has spent his entire life ripping people off. The biggest banks that were considered "too big to fail" at the time of the Recession are even bigger now, and virtually all new income is going to the top one percent. Giving Wall Street another break isn't solving the crisis of income and wealth inequality.
While Paul Ryan and the House were stripping essential banking protections, voters in the United Kingdom rejected the Conservative government and their cabal of bankers and billionaires, and embraced Jeremy Corbyn and the Labour Party's reinvigorated progressive movement. People across the world are ready for a movement that takes on the greed of the few, and works on behalf of the many.
Glass-Steagall is part of the solution, but it doesn't solve the entire problem. We've still got so much work left ahead, like overturning Citizens United to get Wall Street and special interest money out of politics. Nobody could have prepared for the daily assaults on our health care, housing, environment, and civil rights since Trump took office, but organizing as many people as we can is the only shot we have at fighting back against the greed he serves. [**Stand with us as we take on Wall Street and the special interests that Congress represents. Sign our petition to let Congress know we won't let Wall Street take us back into another recession.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0daf/43f17890/d08b5ea1/62037aa6/2389331383/VEsE/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/glass-steagall?source=em170609-full")
We will see economic justice be done in this country, with an economy that works for all. Thank you for joining us.
In solidarity,
Shannon Jackson
Executive Director
Our Revolution
